history.ancient: The Chechens are indigenous to the North Caucasus. Genetic and linguistic evidence links them to the Hurrian and Urartian civilizations.
history.middle_ages: The Kingdom of Alania and the resistance against Mongol invasions. Adoption of Islam from the 16th to 19th centuries.
history.caucasian_wars: 19th-century resistance led by Sheikh Mansur and later Imam Shamil against the Russian Empire. The struggle for independence survived centuries.
history.deportation_1944: Operation Lentil (Aardakh) - The forced relocation of the entire Chechen and Ingush population to Central Asia by Stalin. Nearly half the population perished. Return was permitted in 1957.
history.modern_conflicts: First Chechen War (1994–1996) and Second Chechen War (1999–2009). A period of immense suffering followed by the current reconstruction phase.

leaders.name: Johar Dudayev
leaders.role: First President of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria

culture.code_of_honor: Konakhalla - The ethical code of a 'Konakh' (noble man), prioritizing courage, hospitality, and protection of the weak.
culture.social_structure: Teip (Clans) and Tukhum (Alliances). There are over 100 teips that form the backbone of Chechen social logic.
culture.language: Chechen (Nokhchiin Mott) - Part of the Northeast Caucasian language family.
culture.dance: Lezginka - A traditional dance symbolizing pride and energy.
culture.hospitality: Sacred tradition. A guest is considered 'sent by God' and must be protected at all costs.

religion.traditions: Deeply influenced by Sufism (Qadiriya and Naqshbandiya tariqas), featuring the 'Zikr' ritual.

geography.landscape: High mountains (Caucasus), fertile valleys, and rivers like Sunzha and Terek.
